items.headline: Veen's 3-double day extends huge hitting streak at Triple-A

news_items.headline: Sources: Cubs acquiring LHP Peterson from Mets
news_items.summary: The Chicago Cubs are acquiring left-hander David Peterson from the New York Mets, sources told ESPN's Jeff Passan.

news_items.headline: Cubs' Swanson ups RBI total to 15 in Mets series
news_items.summary: Dansby Swanson had 11 RBI in the Cubs' doubleheader sweep of the Mets, breaking the franchise record of 10 set by Hall of Famer Ron Santo on July 6, 1970.

news_items.headline: Cardinals elevate DeWitt to chief executive office...
news_items.summary: Bill DeWitt III was named chief executive officer of the St. Louis Cardinals on Wednesday after serving as team president since 2008.

news_items.headline: Pirates' Horwitz exits early with hamstring injury...
news_items.summary: Pirates first baseman Spencer Horwitz left in the fourth inning against Seattle with discomfort in his left hamstring Wednesday night.

news_items.headline: Twins' Abel set for arthroscopic elbow surgery
news_items.summary: Twins right-hander Mick Abel has been recommended for arthroscopic elbow surgery after a recent setback in his injury recovery. The timetable for Abel's return won't be determined until after the surgery.

news_items.headline: Yankees put McMahon on IL with throat infection
news_items.summary: The Yankees put infielder Ryan McMahon on the 10-day injured list due to a throat infection and recalled utility player Oswaldo Cabrera from Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.

news_items.headline: Brewers' Uribe to serve ban month after actions
news_items.summary: Brewers reliever Abner Uribe plans to serve his one-game suspension Wednesday night, nearly a month after Major League Baseball handed down the penalty.

news_items.headline: Devers apologized to Vitello: 'A misunderstanding'...
news_items.summary: Giants first baseman Rafael Devers apologized to manager Tony Vitello and said his actions at first base in the ninth inning of San Francisco's one-run loss to Miami on Sunday are being "blown out of proportion."

news_items.headline: Hill's a hero! Down to last strike, Phils rally for 2nd straight comeback win
Mookie Betts continues resurgent streak with 300th career homer in Dodgers win
news_items.summary: Mookie Betts is starting to look like Mookie Betts again.

news_items.headline: Mets' Marcus Semien laments 'tough day' against Cubs as defensive errors begin to pile up
news_items.summary: Marcus Semien committed two errors in the night cap of the Mets' loss to the Cubs on Wednesday night and lamented the overall "tough day" he and the team had.

news_items.headline: Mets trading David Peterson to Cubs
news_items.summary: The Mets are trading LHP David Peterson, the team's longest-tenured player, to the Chicago Cubs for minor league infielder Cole Mathis.

news_items.headline: How does Goldy (2 HRs) keep dominating Skubal? 'I have no idea'
Cubs Minor League Wrap: Jefferson Rojas homers twice in Smokies rout
news_items.summary: Jefferson Rojas, Josiah Hartshorn, Kevin Alcanatara all hit home runs today.

news_items.headline: Phillies stun Nationals with another miraculous ninth-inning comeback
news_items.summary: The Nationals had the Phillies down to their final strike. Again. The Phillies won. Again.

news_items.headline: Braves sleepwalk in San Diego and get swept
news_items.summary: There are many highs and many lows over the course of a 162 game baseball season. And this sure feels about as low as you can get. The Braves continued their spiral on Wednesday night in San Diego, falling by a score of 5-2. It was their fourth loss in a row to drop them […]
